item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Enhancements to maintenance notifications in Azure Service Health
item.description: We’re introducing regional level consolidated maintenance notifications for Azure Database for PostgreSQL to simplify how you stay informed about upcoming maintenance. Instead of one notification per server, now you receive a single notification per regio

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: PgBouncer 1.25.1 support in Azure Database for PostgreSQL – Flexible Server
item.description: PgBouncer 1.25.1 is now generally available in Azure Database for PostgreSQL. As a built-in connection pooling feature, PgBouncer helps you scale to thousands of connections with low overhead by efficiently managing idle and short-lived connections.With t

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Observability in AKS Namespace and Workload Views
item.description: AKS now surfaces observability data powered by Azure Monitor managed service for Prometheus directly within Namespace and Workload views, making it easier to:Monitor cluster and workload healthTroubleshoot pending or failed podsAnalyze resource utilizatio

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Files assessments are now available using Azure Migrate
item.description: Azure Migrate now supports assessments for Azure Files, allowing customers and partners to efficiently plan migrations of on-premises SMB and NFS file shares (Windows and Linux) to Azure Files. This feature enables discovery of file shares, visibility int

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift adds NVIDIA H100 and H200 GPU support
item.description: Azure Red Hat OpenShift (ARO) now supports NVIDIA H100 and H200 GPU-based Azure Virtual Machine SKUs, enabling customers to run large-scale AI, machine learning, and high-performance computing (HPC) workloads on a fully managed OpenShift service. This upd

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Disable HTTP proxy in AKS
item.description: Organizations that use HTTP proxies to control outbound traffic often need to change or remove those settings as network requirements evolve, which can be disruptive in running clusters. Azure Kubernetes Service allows administrators to disable HTTP proxy

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions now supports MCP resource triggers
item.description: Developers building MCP servers on Azure Functions can expose tools but not resources. Now, Azure Functions supports MCP resource triggers, enabling developers to expose resources directly from an Azure Functions–hosted Model Context Protocol (MCP) server

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Pod CIDR expansion in AKS
item.description: When clusters exhaust available pod IP addresses, teams have historically been forced to rebuild environments to restore capacity, creating operational disruption. Pod CIDR expansion allows overlay‑based AKS clusters to increase their pod IP range in plac

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Network Security Perimeter for Azure Service Bus
item.description: Network Security Perimeter (NSP) support for Azure Service Bus is now generally available. Network Security Perimeter creates a logical network boundary around your Service Bus namespaces and other Azure PaaS resources, blocking unauthorized public access

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Rule impact analysis on Azure Network Watcher
item.description: Rule impact analysis on Azure Network Watcher enables you to preview the impact of security admin rules before applying them to your environment. This feature helps you validate rule behavior, identify potential conflicts, and ensure that connectivity req

item.title: Retirement: Python support for Azure App Service and Azure Functions on Windows will be retired on March 31, 2027
item.description: Starting March 31, 2027, Python applications hosted on Azure App Service on Windows and Azure Functions on Windows will no longer run. After this date: Your application configuration and content will remain intact Your apps will stop running

item.title: Retirement: AzureDnsEndpoints for Azure storage accounts will be retired March 2027
item.description: AzureDnsZone endpoints (preview) for Azure Blob storage accounts will enter retirement in March 2027. Customers leveraging AzureDnsZone endpoints (preview) should transition to Standard endpoints for all new and existing blob storage account deployments b

item.title: Retirement: Service Fabric support for Windows Server 2022 ends on March 31, 2027 - upgrade to Windows Server 2025
item.description: Service Fabric will end support for all clusters running on Windows Server 2022 on March 31, 2027. Customers must upgrade to Windows Server 2025 by this date to remain in a supported state. This change aligns Service Fabric’s Windows OS image support mode

item.title: Retirement: Service Fabric support for Windows Server 2019 ends on March 31, 2027 - upgrade to Windows Server 2025
item.description: Service Fabric will end support for all clusters running on Windows Server 2019 on March 31, 2027. Customers must upgrade to Windows Server 2025 by this date to remain in a supported state. This change aligns Service Fabric’s Windows OS image support mode

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Unlock Client-Side Configuration at Scale with Azure App Configuration and Azure Front Door
item.description: You can now deliver dynamic configuration directly to client-side applications—securely and at CDN scale—thanks to Azure App Configuration’s integration with Azure Front Door. This delivers new flexibility for modern apps and unlocks a critical capability

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Data Box enhances compliance with automatic Secure Erasure Certificates
item.description: Azure Data Box now automatically generates a downloadable Secure Erasure Certificate for every completed order, providing verification that all data on the device has been securely erased in accordance with NIST 800-88 Revision 2 standards. The certific

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Data Box now supports Azure Files Provisioned v2
item.description: Azure Data Box now supports ingestion of data into Azure Files Provisioned v2 storage accounts. Azure Files Provisioned v2 is a billing model where customers provision and pay for specific capacity, IOPS, and throughput values; giving greater flexibility

item.title: [In development] Public Preview: Azure NetApp Files storage with cool access enhancement
item.description: QoS update with cool access enabled on Premium and Ultra service levels improves how Azure NetApp Files balances performance and cost for mixed hot and cool workloads. Throughput automatically adjusts as data moves to cool storage, maintaining hot‑tier pe

item.title: Retirement: Select Azure AI Language Features
item.description: On March 20, 2029, Microsoft will retire the following eight Azure AI Language features: Key Phrase ExtractionSentiment Analysis and Opinion MiningCustom Text ClassificationConversational Language Understanding (CLU)Custom Question Answering (CQA)Orchestr

item.title: Retirement: External Data Import & Data Connections in Azure Machine Learning will be retired on September 30, 2026
item.description: Import data from external sources (Preview) – S3, Snowflake, Azure SQL Db along with external Data Connections (Preview) in Azure Machine Learning are being deprecated. These features will no longer be available after September 30, 2026. What this means

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure DevOps March 2026 updates
item.description: The following updates were made to Azure DevOps in March 2026. The Remote MCP Server entered public preview, enabling seamless AI-powered integration with Azure DevOps without requiring a local server. Security improvements included

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Event Grid provides new capabilities for smarter, more secure event-driven architectures
item.description: Azure Event Grid offers a variety of features in Public Preview – intended to help you better manage large-scale, distributed systems with improved interoperability and control. Public Preview Features: Cross-Tenant Webhook Delivery: Enables secu

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: User delegation SAS for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues
item.description: User delegation SAS for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues is now generally available. User delegation SAS allows users to create a more secure SAS token than account or service SAS by tying the SAS token to the delegator. This extension of UDK

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Event Grid provides new capabilities for smarter, more secure event-driven architectures
item.description: Azure Event Grid now offers a variety of new features intended to help you better manage large-scale, distributed systems with improved interoperability and control. Generally Available features: MQTT Message Ordering: Ensures in-order message de

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Azure now available from new cloud region in Denmark
item.description: Microsoft announces the opening of its cloud region in Denmark to accelerate digital transformation and AI innovation. The Denmark East region provides Danish customers with local, secure cloud infrastructure, supporting local data residency, low latency,

item.title: Retirement: Manually registered Azure VPN Clients for ​Azure Government and ​Microsoft Azure operated by 21Vianet clouds will be retired on March 31, 2029
item.description: On March 31, 2029, we’ll be retiring manually registered Azure VPN clients for point-to-site connections with Microsoft Entra ID authentication for ​Azure Government and ​Microsoft Azure operated by 21Vianet clouds. Recommended action: To prevent disrupt

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in US Gov Arizona
item.description: Azure Premium SSD v2 is now available in US Gov Arizona (a region without Availability Zone). Azure Premium SSD v2 disk, a next-generation general-purpose block storage option for Azure virtual machines, offers sub-millisecond latencies and exceptional p

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Ephemeral OS Disk with full caching for VM/VMSS
item.description: Ephemeral OS Disk with full caching is now available in public preview for Azure VM/VMSS, bringing significantly faster and more reliable OS disk performance to your workloads. By caching the entire OS disk image on local VM storage (cache disk, resource

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift in Indonesia Central
item.description: Azure Red Hat OpenShift (ARO) is now generally available in the Indonesia Central Azure region, expanding regional availability for customers running mission‑critical OpenShift workloads on Azure. This release enables organizations in Indonesia and the br

item.title: Retirement: Deprecation of sidecar for remote-write for Azure Monitor managed service for Prometheus
item.description: On March 31, 2027 Azure Monitor will be deprecating the sidecar for remote-write of Prometheus metrics to Azure Monitor Workspace. This is as part of our ongoing efforts to improve reliability and reduce complexity.We recommend configuring your self-hoste

item.title: Retirement: Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL will retire on March 31, 2029
item.description: On March 31, 2029, Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL will be retired, and you'll need to migrate to Azure Database for PostgreSQL Elastic Cluster by that date. Azure Database for PostgreSQL Elastic Cluster delivers the same distributed PostgreSQL capabilitie

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in South India
item.description: Azure Premium SSD v2 is now available in South India (a region without Availability Zone).Azure Premium SSD v2 disk, a next-generation general-purpose block storage option for Azure virtual machines, offers sub-millisecond latencies and exceptional price

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure SQL updates for late-March 2026
item.description: In late-March 2026, the following updates and enhancements were made to Azure SQL: Configure built-in SQL code analysis rules and severity settings without editing project XML. Use Fabric connectivity and provisioning options to connect wi

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Cosmos DB Mirroring in Microsoft Fabric with private endpoints
item.description: We're excited to announce that private endpoint support for Azure Cosmos DB Mirroring in Microsoft Fabric is now generally available. You can now unlock powerful analytics on your operational data while keeping your enhanced network security posture intac

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Blue-green agent pool upgrade in AKS
item.description: In‑place node pool upgrades can introduce risk by applying changes directly to running environments. Blue‑green agent pool upgrades create a parallel node pool with the new configuration, allowing validation before workloads are shifted and providing a cl

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Fabric Mirroring integration for Azure Database for MySQL
item.description: We are excited to announce the public preview of Fabric Mirroring integration for Azure Database for MySQL – Flexible Server. You can now replicate MySQL operational data into Microsoft Fabric in near real time, without building or maintaining ETL pipelin

item.title: [Launched] Public Preview: Azure SQL Managed Instance change event streaming
item.description: You can now stream row‑level data changes—inserts, updates, and deletes—from Azure SQL Managed Instance to Azure Event Hubs in near real time with change event streaming (CES). SQL publishes changes as transactions commit, reducing latency while minimizin

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Custom time zone support for pg_cron via cron.timezone in Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: You can now modify the cron.timezone server parameter in Azure Database for PostgreSQL. This parameter controls the time zone used by pg_cron when evaluating scheduled jobs. By configuring cron.timezone, you can ensure scheduled jobs run according to your

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: PostgreSQL migration service supports compatible EDB workloads into Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: We’re excited to announce that EDB PostgreSQL is now supported as a source for migration into Azure Database for PostgreSQL. You can migrate and consolidate PostgreSQL estates from EDB Postgres Extended Server to Azure using secure, reliable workflows des

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: PostgreSQL migration service supports for Google AlloyDB into Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: We’re excited to announce that Google AlloyDB is now supported as a source for migration into Azure Database for PostgreSQL. You can migrate and consolidate PostgreSQL estates from Google AlloyDB to Azure using secure, reliable workflows designed for mini

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Online migration now uses the pgoutput plugin
item.description: You can now leverage pgoutput for online (minimal-downtime) migrations with improved reliability and performance. By aligning with PostgreSQL’s native logical replication framework, this update improves ecosystem compatibility with modern PostgreSQL deplo

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Container network metrics filtering for AKS
item.description: Network observability can generate large volumes of metrics, making it difficult for teams to focus on data that is operationally relevant. Container network metrics filtering in Azure Container Networking Services (ACNS) allows operators to control which

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: AI Agent for container networking troubleshooting
item.description: Troubleshooting Kubernetes networking issues is often slowed by logs and metrics scattered across multiple tools, forcing engineers to manually correlate signals during incidents. The container networking agent provides a lightweight, web‑based interface

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: AKS managed GPU metrics in Azure Monitor
item.description: Teams running GPU‑backed workloads often lack integrated visibility into GPU utilization alongside Kubernetes metrics. AKS managed GPU metrics automatically expose performance and utilization data from NVIDIA GPU‑enabled node pools into managed Prometheus

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Cross-cluster networking in Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager
item.description: Organizations running applications across multiple Kubernetes clusters often face challenges with performance, global service discovery, and observability due to the complexity of distributed microservice environments. Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager now p

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Container Storage v2.1.0 now with Elastic SAN integration and on demand installation
item.description: Containerized workloads often require higher and more consistent storage performance without managing large numbers of individual disks. Azure Container Storage integration with Elastic SAN allows Kubernetes clusters to consume storage from a shared pool

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Container network logs in AKS
item.description: Networking issues in Kubernetes environments can be difficult to diagnose due to limited visibility into traffic flows and insufficient context around failures. Container network logs in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), now generally available, provide con

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Monitor Prometheus community recommended alerts for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes
item.description: Azure Monitor now offers one-click enablement of Prometheus recommended alerts directly in the Azure Portal for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes clusters. These alerts, based on enhanced Prometheus community rules, provide comprehensive coverage across cluste

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Application routing with meshless Istio in AKS
item.description: Following the deprecation of ingress‑nginx, Kubernetes operators need a supported, standards‑aligned migration path for ingress without the complexity of a full service mesh. Application Routing with Meshless Istio enables adoption of the Kubernetes Gatew

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Microsoft Azure Kubernetes Application Network
item.description: As Kubernetes environments scale across regions and clusters, IP‑based networking becomes difficult to manage and provides limited application‑level visibility and security controls. Azure Kubernetes Application Network introduces application‑layer abstra

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Ingest OTLP data into Azure Monitor with the OpenTelemetry Collector
item.description: Azure Monitor now supports native ingestion of OpenTelemetry Protocol (OTLP) signals, enabling you to send telemetry data directly from OpenTelemetry-instrumented applications and platforms to Azure Monitor. You can configure your OpenTelemetry Collector

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL updates for mid-March 2026
item.description: In mid-March 2026, the following updates and enhancements were made to Azure SQL: Reduce the consumption of storage space, I/O, and memory and improve performance without investing time and effort into index maintenance jobs. Configure

item.title: Retirement: Azure SQL Elastic query - Shard_Map_Manager mode
item.description: Starting March 31, 2027 Elastic query using Shard_Map_Manager external data source type reaches end of support.Existing workloads can continue to run using Azure SQL Elastic query - Shard_Map_Manager mode, but will no longer receive updates or support fro

item.title: Retirement: Azure Sphere will be retired on July 31, 2031
item.description: The Azure Sphere service is retiring and will stop supporting all customer application, OS, bug and security updates, along with DAA certificate issuance, on July 31, 2031. Why is this happening? Azure Sphere pioneered secure IoT connectivity, combining

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Databricks OneLake Catalog Federation
item.description: You can now enable OneLake catalog federation in Azure Databricks to query Microsoft Fabric OneLake data without copying or moving it. By connecting Unity Catalog to a OneLake catalog, Azure Databricks automatically syncs schemas and tables so Fabric Lake

item.title: Retirement: HC-series Azure Virtual Machines will be retired on May 31, 2027
item.description: On May 31, 2027, Microsoft Azure will retire the following HC-series virtual machine (VMs) sizes: Standard_HC44rs, Standard_HC44-16rs, and Standard_HC44-32rs. To ensure continuity and improved performance, we recommend transitioning to one of the current‑

item.title: Retirement: HBv2-series Azure Virtual Machines will be retired on May 31, 2027
item.description: On May 31, 2027, Microsoft Azure will retire the following HBv2-series virtual machine (VMs) sizes: Standard_HB120rs_v2, Standard_HB120-96rs_v2, Standard_HB120-64rs_v2, Standard_HB120-32rs_v2, and Standard_HB120-16rs_v2. To ensure continuity and improved

item.title: Retirement: NP-series Azure Virtual Machines will be retired on May 31, 2027
item.description: On May 31, 2027, Microsoft Azure will retire the follow NP-series virtual machine (VMs) sizes: Standard_NP10s, Standard_NP20s, and Standard_NP40s. To ensure continuity and optimal performance, we recommend transitioning to one of the following Azure GPU V

item.title: Retirement: Emissions Impact Dashboard for Azure
item.description: Effective March 31, 2027, the Emissions Impact Dashboard for Azure hosted by Power BI will be retired. After this date, the dashboard will no longer be accessible and technical support will be discontinued. We strongly encourage customers to export their

item.title: [Launched] General availability: Azure SQL updates for mid-March 2026
item.description: In mid-March 2026, the following updates and enhancements were made to Azure SQL: Publish SQL database projects directly from Visual Studio Code to streamline database deployment workflows. View and edit SQL table data directly in the MSSQ

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift Managed Identity and Workload Identity
item.description: Azure Red Hat OpenShift now supports managed identities and workload identities as a generally available capability, enabling you to run OpenShift clusters and applications on Azure without long lived service principal credentials. This aligns Azure Red H

item.title: [In preview] Public preview: GitHub Copilot integration in Schema Designer for the MSSQL extension
item.description: The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code now introduces GitHub Copilot integration in Schema Designer, expanding the visual schema design experience with AI-assisted capabilities. Based on developer feedback and evolving workflows, you can now understan

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Database DevOps in SSMS powered by SQL projects
item.description: Bring the benefits of schema-as-code to your database development workflow. SQL database projects enable you to source control your database schema, perform reliable deployments to any environment, and integrate code quality checks into your development p

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: SQL MCP Server
item.description: Announcing SQL MCP Server, our Model Context Protocol (MCP) connector for production databases. SQL MCP Server is a feature rich component of data API builder (DAB) that gives you a simple, predictable, secure way to bring AI agents into your data workflo

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: GitHub Copilot in SQL Server Management Studio 22
item.description: GitHub Copilot is now generally available in SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS) 22, bringing AI‑powered assistance directly into the application that you use every day. With GitHub Copilot in SSMS, you can use natural language to develop, explain, fix,

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Lakeflow Connect Free Tier now available in Azure Databricks
item.description: Azure Databricks now offers a Lakeflow Connect Free Tier that gives each workspace 100 free DBUs per workspace per day for ingestion from SaaS applications and databases. This allows you to bring roughly 100 million records of data per workspace per day i

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Versionless key support for transparent data encryption in Azure SQL Database
item.description: Azure SQL Database now supports versionless keys for transparent data encryption (TDE), giving you a simpler and more reliable way to manage your own encryption keys. You no longer need to reference a specific key version from Azure Key Vault or Managed H

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Data API builder with built-in GitHub Copilot in MSSQL extension
item.description: The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code now introduces Data API builder in public preview with built-in GitHub Copilot integration, bringing a guided back-end generation experience directly into your development workflow. You can generate REST, GraphQL

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Default Rule Set 2.2 and updates to ruleset support policy
item.description: We are updating the managed ruleset support policy for Azure Web Application Firewall (WAF) following the general availability of Default Rule Set (DRS) 2.2 on Azure Application Gateway and Azure Front Door. This update provides a clearer, more predictabl

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Foundry Agent Service
item.description: Announcing general availability (GA) of the next-gen Foundry Agent Service—a redesigned API format and runtime experience designed to help teams build and operate agents that can move from prototype to production with confidence. This GA moment is anchore

item.title: Retirement: Azure VMware Solution AV36P and AV52 node retirement on June 30, 2029
item.description: Microsoft is announcing the retirement of the AV36P and AV52 Nodes on June 30, 2029. The terms of your current AV36P and AV52 Reserved Instance (RI) are not affected by this announcement. At this time, we recommend that you review your current RI expirati

item.title: Retirement: Support for Windows Server 2016 Marketplace images on Azure Batch pools will end on January 12, 2027
item.description: Azure Batch periodically retires support for operating systems that reach end-of-life status. Support for Windows Server 2016 concluded on January 11, 2022, with extended support scheduled to end on January 12, 2027. Marketplace images for Windows Server

item.title: Retirement: Migration of Azure Batch Low-Priority VMs to Spot VMs in early March
item.description: Azure Batch had retired Low-Priority VMs on September 30, 2025 and will begin a system-initiated migration to Spot VMs beginning March 1, 2026. This change aligns Batch with the broader Azure Spot infrastructure and simplifies the platform’s VM offering.

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Entra ID-Based Access for Azure Blob Storage SFTP
item.description: Microsoft Entra ID-based access for Azure Blob Storage SFTP is now in public preview. This new capability empowers you to use Microsoft Entra ID identities (including guest users via Entra External Identities) to securely connect to Azure Blob Storage via

item.title: Retirement: End of life reminder of NVv3 and NVv4-series Azure virtual machine in Azure Batch pools
item.description: Microsoft Azure will retire support for NVv3 or NVv4-series VMs on September 30th, 2026, including NVv3 Series (NVIDIA Tesla M60 GPU): Standard_NV12s_v3, Standard_NV12hs_v3, Standard_NV24s_v3, Standard_NV24ms_v3, Standard_NV32ms_v3, Standard_NV48s_v3 NVv4

item.title: Retirement: Flatcar Container Linux for AKS (preview)
item.description: Azure Kubernetes Service support for Flatcar Container Linux for AKS (preview) will be retired on June 8, 2026, transition to a supported alternative by that date. From now to June 7, 2026, you can continue to use Flatcar Container Linux for AKS (preview)

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Summary rules in Log Analytics workspace now support manual “Retry bin”
item.description: Summary rules run batch aggregation inside your Log Analytics workspace and re-ingest the summarized results into a custom destination table. When a bin fails, that can create gaps in your summarized dataset. “Retry bin” helps you remediate bin-level fail

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Storage Mover enables private data transfers from AWS S3 to Azure Blob
item.description: Azure Storage Mover now enables direct, secure data migration from AWS S3 data in Virtual Private Cloud to Azure Blob Storage with private networking. This update removes the need for manual pipelines or third-party tools, supports automation through the

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Terraform, Bicep, Ansible support for elastic clusters on Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: You can now provision and manage Azure Database for PostgreSQL elastic clusters using Terraform, Bicep, and Ansible, with full General Availability support. With native infrastructure-as-code support, you can consistently create, scale, and manage elastic

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Database for PostgreSQL dashboards with Grafana
item.description: You can now monitor your Azure Database for PostgreSQL with rich, built-in Grafana dashboards—right from the Azure portal. This native integration with Azure Monitor means you no longer need to set up or manage a separate Grafana instance to get deep visi

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Customer‑managed encryption keys now supported on Premium SSD v2 disks for Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: You can now enhance data security for your Azure Database for PostgreSQL workloads by using customer managed keys (CMKs) with Premium SSD v2 disks. Available in public preview, this update gives you greater control over how your data at rest is encrypted,

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure SRE Agent with new capabilities
item.description: Azure SRE Agent is now generally available. This AI-powered operations agent helps teams improve uptime, reduce incident impact, and cut operational toil by accelerating diagnosis and automating response workflows. The GA release introduces deep context g

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Query Profiler in MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code
item.description: The MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code now introduces Query Profiler in public preview, bringing real-time insight into your database activity directly inside Visual Studio Code. With Query Profiler, you can capture query and database activity as it h

item.title: Retirement: Azure Policy faster enforcement and retirement of login/logout workaround
item.description: Over the years, we’ve made significant investments to improve the responsiveness of the Azure Policy service. As a result, policy assignment creation and updates for Resource Manager mode policies now get enforced within 5 minutes.In light of cache refres

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Draft & Deploy on Azure Firewall
item.description: The new Draft & Deploy feature for Azure Firewall Policy introduces a streamlined, two-phase approach to managing firewall policies, significantly reducing deployment time and disruption. Traditionally, any policy update would trigger a full deployment of

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Databricks update workspace network configuration
item.description: Azure Databricks workspaces have two network configurations – Azure Databricks-managed VNet and VNet Injection. The ability to update the Network Configuration of your workspace is now generally available. This enhancement provides greater flexibility and

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Databricks Lakebase
item.description: Today we’re announcing the General Availability of Azure Databricks Lakebase, a managed PostgreSQL environment with next generation separation of storage and compute for OLTP, providing instant availability, instant clones, and scale-to-zero. Lakebase let

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk Storage is now available in a third Availability Zone in New Zealand North
item.description: Azure Premium SSD v2 is now available in a third Availability Zone in New Zealand North. Azure Premium SSD v2 disk, a next-generation general-purpose block storage option for Azure virtual machines, offers sub-millisecond latencies and exceptional pric

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: DCesv6, DCedsv6, ECesv6, and ECedsv6 confidential VMs
item.description: The DCesv6, DCedsv6, ECesv6, and ECedsv6 series are Azure’s next generation of confidential Virtual Machines (VMs) based on 5th Gen Intel® Xeon® processors with Intel® Trust Domain Extensions (Intel® TDX). These new confidential VMs help organizations to

item.title: Retirement: Managed NGINX Ingress with Application Routing Add-on Retiring November 2026
item.description: The upstream Ingress-NGINX project is being deprecated and will stop receiving updates after March 2026. Microsoft will continue providing critical security patches through 30 November 2026, after which the NGINX ingress controller will no longer be suppo

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift is now available in Malaysia West, New Zealand North, and Mexico Central
item.description: Azure Red Hat OpenShift is now generally available in three additional Azure regions: Malaysia West, New Zealand North, and Mexico Central. This expansion strengthens Azure Red Hat OpenShift’s presence across Asia Pacific and Latin America, enabling custo

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Restrict usage of user delegation SAS to an Entra ID identity
item.description: We’re excited to announce the addition of enhanced secure authentication in Azure Storage that combines the flexibility of user-delegation shared access signature (SAS) with the user-bound access of Entra ID. User-bound user delegation SAS is now in previ

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Secure ingestion and pod placement for Azure Monitor pipeline
item.description: We are announcing several capabilities in public preview with Azure Monitor pipeline. Secure Ingress to Azure Monitor pipeline from external endpoints (TLS/mTLS) with Bring Your Own Certificates: BYOC empowers enterprises to maintain contro

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure SQL updates for late-February 2026
item.description: In late-February 2026, the following updates and enhancements were made to Azure SQL: Introducing Search Database Objects in the MSSQL extension for Visual Studio Code. Instantly find tables, views, functions, and procedures with full scripti

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL updates for late-February 2026
item.description: In late-February 2026, the following updates and enhancements were made to Azure SQL: Manage your databases directly from the dialog using actions such as Name, Rename, and Drop. Import a flat file to your database in the MSSQL Extension fo

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Geo‑redundant backups for Premium SSD v2 in Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: You can now configure geo‑redundant backups with Premium SSD v2 disks for Azure Database for PostgreSQL, giving you an additional layer of disaster recovery for mission‑critical workloads. With this release, your automated backups are securely stored in a

item.title: Retirement: Windows Server Annual Channel (preview)
item.description: Windows Server Annual Channel (Preview) on AKS will be retired on May 15, 2026, please transition to Long Term Servicing Channel (LTSC) by that date. We encourage you to transition to Long Term Servicing Channel (LTSC) prior to the retirement date to exp

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: WAF Insights for Application Gateway
item.description: Announcing the public preview of Application Gateway WAF Insights. Azure Application Gateway WAF now offers WAF Insights, providing an interactive view of logs and metrics. WAF Insights enables quick investigation of blocked requests, analysis of attack p

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in Brazil Southeast, and in a third Availability Zone in Malaysia West and Indonesia Central
item.description: Azure Premium SSD v2 is now available in Brazil Southeast (a region without Availability Zone), and in a third Availability Zone in Malaysia West and Indonesia Central.Azure Premium SSD v2 disk, a next-generation general-purpose block storage option for A

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6 is now available on Azure Databricks
item.description: Azure Databricks now supports Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6 through Azure Databricks AI Model Serving. Claude Sonnet 4.6 delivers frontier-level performance on complex coding, agentic workflows, and professional knowledge work including reasoning at a lower

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Unified tooling in the AKS MCP server
item.description: Managing AKS MCP servers can be affected by the number of tools and the amount of context required, which can increase latency and cost. The unified tooling for the AKS MCP Server introduces two new tools in public preview, call_az and call_kubectl, that

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Cluster mode for the agentic CLI for AKS
item.description: Users often face security and manageability challenges when diagnostic agents inherit broad user permissions, such as cluster-admin rights, and when each user must deploy their own instance. Cluster mode for the agentic CLI for AKS, now in public preview,

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Quota and deployment troubleshooting tools for Azure Functions Flex Consumption
item.description: Azure Functions Flex Consumption introduces new platform‑integrated tools that provide clearer insight into quota availability and deployment behavior. The quota troubleshooting experience surfaces Flex Consumption quota limits and related constraints,

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions .NET 10 support
item.description: Azure Functions support for .NET 10 is generally available, enabling production use of the latest .NET runtime for serverless applications. This support was introduced as part of the Azure Functions Ignite 2025 updates. .NET 10 is supported using the iso

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Node auto-provisioning support in Sovereign Cloud
item.description: Node auto‑provisioning removes the need for teams to manually size and manage compute resources for Kubernetes workloads. Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) now provides general availability support for node auto‑provisioning in Sovereign Cloud. This capabili

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Node auto-provisioning enabled clusters in AKS now support LocalDNS
item.description: Customers previously could not enable LocalDNS on clusters that used Node auto‑provisioning in AKS, limiting their ability to use this feature in those environments. LocalDNS is now generally available for AKS clusters with Node auto‑provisioning, providi

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Encryption at host & disk encryption sets support in node auto-provisioning
item.description: Node auto-provisioning enabled clusters previously did not have support for Encryption at Host and disk encryption sets. This limited users with certain security requirements for their cluster to not be able to use node auto-provisioning. Node auto-pro

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Instant access support for incremental snapshots of Azure Premium SSD v2 and Ultra Disk
item.description: Announcing the General Availability of instant access support for incremental snapshots of Premium SSD v2 (Pv2) and Ultra Disk. This new capability enables you to restore new disks immediately after creating snapshots of Pv2 and Ultra disks. Newly restore

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: AKS support for Kubernetes version 1.34
item.description: Kubernetes version 1.34 is now generally available for AKS. This release introduces 58 upstream enhancements, including 23 Stable features, 22 Beta features, and 13 Alpha features.These enhancements help technical practitioners improve cluster operations,

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL updates for mid-February 2026
item.description: In mid-February 2026, the following updates and enhancements were made to Azure SQL:Simplify SQL deployments from Visual Studio Code using the new SQL Projects Publish dialog.Connections Migration dialog enables you to import your existing workflows and c

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Front Door Premium now supports Azure Private Link origins in UAE North
item.description: You can now set UAE North as a region for Azure Private Link-enabled origins in your Azure Front Door Premium profile. Azure Private Link-enabled origins in Azure Front Door allow you to deliver content to your end-users through public Azure Front Door en

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Databricks Supervisor Agent
item.description: Introducing Supervisor Agent, now Generally Available in Azure Databricks. Supervisor Agent is part of the Azure Databricks Agent Bricks Platform. Supervisor Agent brings a breakthrough in agentic intelligence—using a dynamic supervisor pattern that under

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Application Gateway for Containers - AKS managed add-on
item.description: We're announcing public preview of an AKS managed add-on that simplifies the deployment and lifecycle management of Application Gateway for Containers and its ALB Controller. With the managed AKS add-on, you can enable Application Gateway for Containers w

item.title: [In development] Private Preview: Vaulted Backups for Azure Disk
item.description: Today, Azure Disk Backup protects data by taking regular, crash-consistent snapshots of Azure Disks. These snapshots are stored within your subscription and tenant in a resource group, referred to as the Operational Tier in Azure Backup. This enables fast

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Monitor pipeline transformations
item.description: Azure Monitor pipeline transformations feature is now Public Preview! This feature enables users to shape telemetry before it’s ingested into Azure Monitor, helping them control ingestion costs, improve data quality, and simplify analytics at scale.Built

item.title: [Launched] ​Generally Available: Claude Opus 4.6 now available on Azure Databricks
item.description: Azure Databricks now supports Anthropic Claude Opus 4.6 through Mosaic AI Model serving. Claude Opus 4.6 is Anthropic's most advanced model, delivering state-of-the-art performance on agentic coding, complex reasoning, and knowledge work tasks. The model

item.title: [In development] Private Preview: New planned datacenter region in Thailand (Thailand South)
item.description: Microsoft is announcing our intent to establish a new datacenter region in Thailand. The datacenter region will expand the availability of Microsoft’s hyperscale cloud services in the region, facilitating enterprise-grade reliability, performance, and com

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: AMD v6 confidential VMs in new regions for January 2026
item.description: DCa/ECa v6 series AMD based confidential virtual machines are now generally available in 11 new regions: Canada Central, Canada East, Norway East, Norway West, Italy North, Germany North, France South, Australia East, West US, West US 3, Germany West Cent

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure virtual network routing appliance
item.description: Azure Virtual Network routing appliance offers private connectivity for workloads across virtual networks. Using specialized hardware, it delivers low latency and high throughput, and optimal performance compared to virtual machines. Deployed into priva

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Claude Opus 4.6, Available on Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Claude Opus 4.6 is now available in Microsoft Foundry, bringing Anthropic’s most advanced reasoning model to a secure, enterprise-ready platform built on Azure. With support for complex coding, knowledge work, and agent-driven workflows, including long-co

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure NetApp Files Elastic zone-redundant service level
item.description: Azure NetApp Files Elastic zone-redundant storage (ANF Elastic ZRS) is an advanced high-availability service level for Azure NetApp Files that provides continuous data access with zero data loss, even if an entire Azure Availability Zone goes offline. Bui

item.title: [In development] Public Preview: X-Forwarded-For (XFF) grouping for rate limiting on Application Gateway WAF v2
item.description: Application Gateway Web Application Firewall (WAF) v2 now supports additional rate‑limiting GroupBy options based on the X‑Forwarded‑For (XFF) HTTP header. This enables customers running Application Gateway behind proxies or CDNs to apply rate limits usin

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Container Storage v2.1.0 now with Elastic SAN integration and on demand installation
item.description: Azure Container Storage v2.1.0 is now generally available. This release adds native Elastic SAN support and introduces a lightweight, on-demand installation model to simplify deployment and operations for Kubernetes workloads on Azure. Elastic SAN Storage

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager namespace-scoped resource placement
item.description: Namespace-scoped resource placement, now in preview for Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager, delivers fine‑grained control over selection and propagation of individual namespace‑scoped resources across multiple clusters. This new capability means it is now p

item.title: Retirement: Azure Front Door and Azure CDN profiles will end support for DHE cipher suites on April 1, 2026
item.description: As part of our ongoing commitment to security, Azure Front Door (Standard, Premium, Classic) and Azure CDN from Microsoft (Classic) services will no longer support weak cipher suites TLS_DHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_GCM_SHA384 and TLS_DHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SH

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Serverless workspaces in Azure Databricks
item.description: Serverless workspaces in Azure Databricks are now generally available, offering a fully managed workspace type that comes preconfigured with serverless compute and default storage. They deliver an enterprise-ready SaaS experience with zero infrastructure

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Default Ruleset 2.2 in WAF for Azure Application Gateway
item.description: Announcing the general availability of Default Rule Set (DRS) 2.2 for Web Application Firewall on Azure Application Gateway.The Azure-managed DRS provides active protection against common web vulnerabilities and exploits. It also includes Microsoft Threat

item.title: Retirement: "Send data to Event Hubs & Storage (Preview)" retiring July 31, 2026
item.description: On July 31, 2026, we'll retire preview feature “Send virtual machine client data to Event Hubs and Storage (Preview)” and it'll no longer be supported by Microsoft. You will not be able to create any new data collection rules using this feature, and any e

item.title: Retirement: Support for Python 3.10 ends on October 1, 2026 – upgrade your Azure Functions apps to Python 3.13
item.description: In alignment with the end of community support, on October 1, 2026, support for Python 3.10 in Azure Functions will end. Your apps that are hosted on Functions will continue to run, but security updates and performance optimizations will no longer be avai

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure AMD Turin Dasv7, Easv7, and Fasv7-series Virtual Machines
item.description: The general purpose Dasv7 and Dalsv7, memory-optimized Easv7, and compute-optimized Fasv7, Falsv7, Famsv7 VMs, are now generally available with and without local disk support. These VMs are available in the following Azure regions: Australia East, Central

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Databricks Agent Bricks Knowledge Assistant
item.description: Azure Databricks Agent Bricks knowledge assistant empowers you to create, deploy, and manage AI agents directly within the Azure Databricks platform, using unified data and AI capabilities. This feature simplifies agent development by providing prebuilt c

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Latest PostgreSQL minor versions supported by Azure Database for PostgreSQL – Flexible Server
item.description: PostgreSQL minor versions 18.1, 17.7, 16.11, 15.15, 14.20, and 13.23 are now supported by Azure Database for PostgreSQL – Flexible Server. These minor version upgrades are automatically performed as part of the monthly planned maintenance in Azure Data

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure NetApp Files support in OpenShift Virtualization
item.description: Azure NetApp Files now enables fast virtual machine (VM) provisioning, instant cloning, and live migration in OpenShift Virtualization. It offers seamlessly scalable storage with predictable performance and enterprise data management for VM workloads from

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: 7th generation Intel-based VMs – Dlsv7/Dsv7/Esv7
item.description: Announcing the preview of new Azure Dlsv7/Dsv7 General Purpose and Esv7 Memory Optimized Virtual Machines (VMs) powered by the latest Intel® Xeon® 6 processors (Granite Rapids). The latest Azure Intel-based v7 VMs are designed to power ever increasing com

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Command Launcher for Java
item.description: At Microsoft Ignite 2025, we announced the Public Preview of the Azure Command Launcher for Java, a new JVM launcher optimized specifically for Azure. This tool provides better default ergonomics for Java applications running in containers and virtual mac

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Deployment safeguards – pod security standard support in AKS
item.description: Organizations deploying workloads on Kubernetes often need a consistent way to enforce pod security configurations across clusters. Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) now supports Pod Security Standards within Deployment Safeguards, enabling centralized manag

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: StandardV2 NAT Gateway with zone-redundancy and StandardV2 public IPs
item.description: StandardV2 SKU NAT Gateway is now generally available, delivering enhanced resiliency, greater performance, and dual-stack connectivity, all at the same price as the Standard SKU. StandardV2 Public IP addresses and prefixes are also generally available.

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Reporting in Playwright Workspaces (part of Azure App Testing)
item.description: Reporting capabilities are now live in Playwright Workspaces. The latest update brings you an integrated, flexible and collaborative reporting experience designed to make debugging tests in Playwright Workspaces easier, faster and more efficient. Customer

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Load Testing in Switzerland North
item.description: Azure Load Testing capability in Azure App Testing is now generally available in Switzerland North. Customers can now use Azure Load Testing, a fully managed load-testing service to generate high scale load and run simulations, easily identify performance

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Application volume group for Oracle create data protection volume group (API)
item.description: The functionality of application volume group for Oracle has been expanded with the ability to create data protection volumes in a volume group with the same anti-affinity layout as production volume groups. Customers benefit from this ability for creatin

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure File Sync in Israel Central
item.description: Azure File Sync enables seamless tiering of data from on-premises Windows Servers to Azure Files for hybrid use cases and simplified migration. It also enables you to leverage the performance, flexibility and compatibility of your on-premises File Server

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Ubuntu 24.04 support in AKS
item.description: Customers upgrading AKS clusters often need a clear and flexible path to adopt new OS versions without disrupting workloads. Ubuntu 24.04 is now generally available on AKS starting with Kubernetes version 1.32, with containerd 2.0 enabled by default. It

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: User delegation SAS for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues
item.description: User delegation SAS for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues is now available in public preview. User delegation (UDK) SAS is already generally available for blobs, and this release extends that support to tables, files, and queues.User delegation

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Ubuntu 24.04 support in AKS
item.description: Ubuntu 24.04 is now generally available on AKS in Kubernetes version 1.32 and above. Containerd 2.0 is enabled by default. Ubuntu 24.04 is default with `Ubuntu` OS Sku in kubernetes version 1.35 and above. There are two ways that Ubuntu 24.04 can be enabl

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Cosmos DB Mirroring with Private Endpoints
item.description: Available now in Public Preview, you can configure Azure Cosmos DB accounts with virtual networks or private endpoints to work with Microsoft Fabric mirroring. This feature allows you to preserve your enhanced network security and seamlessly replicate you

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Cloud-native apps on Kubernetes pricing calculator scenario
item.description: The new cloud-native apps on Kubernetes pricing calculator scenario allows teams to estimate the total cost of ownership for a production-ready AKS cluster with other key Azure services, including Azure Container Registry, Azure Monitoring, Microsoft Defe

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Geo-Replication for Azure Service Bus Premium
item.description: The Geo-Replication feature for Azure Service Bus in the premium tier is now generally available. The Service Bus Geo-Replication feature is one of the options to insulate Azure Service Bus applications against outages and disasters, providing replication

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Premium SSD v2 Disk is now available in Austria East and in a second Availability Zone in Japan West
item.description: Azure Premium SSD v2 is now available in Austria East and in a second Availability Zone in Japan West. Azure Premium SSD v2, a next-generation general-purpose block storage solution, offers sub-millisecond latencies and exceptional price performance for

item.title: Retirement: Deprecation of Custom Resource Providers
item.description: In 2026, Azure Resource Manager will be deprecating the Custom Resource Provider (CuRP) service.We will retire the feature by October 31, 2026. We will be performing a scream test on October 27, 2025, as part of this deprecation. On July 31, 2026, creates

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Blob-to-Blob migration made simple with Azure Storage Mover
item.description: Azure Storage Mover’s new Azure Blob container-to-container migration feature is now in Public Preview, empowering organizations to move data between two Blob containers within the same or different storage accounts, subscriptions or Azure regions securel

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Service Bus SDK type bindings in Azure Functions for Node.js
item.description: Azure Functions triggers and bindings enable you to easily integrate event and data sources with function applications. Now that SDK type bindings support for Azure Blob Storage is available, we’re also introducing support for Service Bus SDK type bindi

item.title: [Launched] Generally Avaailable: Azure SQL updates for early December 2025
item.description: In mid-December 2025, the following updates and enhancements were made to Azure SQL: Identify causes that auto-resume serverless workloads using Activity Log in Azure SQL Database to troubleshoot and optimize access patterns. Learn more.

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Use Azure SRE Agent with Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: You can now simplify how you diagnose and resolve issues in your applications running on Azure Cosmos DB with the introduction of the Azure Cosmos DB Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) Agent, built on the Azure SRE Agent platform. This new capability give

item.title: [In preview] Generally Available: Azure NetApp Files cross-zone-region replication (CZRR)
item.description: Cross-zone-region replication builds on the existing capabilities of cross-region replication and cross-zone replication. It enables you to replicate volumes across regions and across availability zones within the same region, combining disaster recovery

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure NetApp Files advanced ransomware protection
item.description: Azure NetApp Files advanced ransomware protection (ANF ARP) is now in Public Preview. ANF ARP is designed to help organizations proactively detect, respond to, and recover from ransomware threats on cloud volumes. ANF ARP monitors Azure NetApp Files volu

item.title: Update: The retirement date for default outbound access has been extended to March 31, 2026.
item.description: The retirement date for default outbound access has been extended to March 31, 2026. This change aligns with the broader Azure Virtual Network (VNet) update and replaces the previously communicated September 30, 2025 date. Starting March 31, 2026, newly c

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Databricks Dashboard subscriptions now support Microsoft Teams
item.description: You can now receive insights where collaboration happens—in Microsoft Teams—with expanded dashboard subscription capabilities in Azure Databricks. This enhancement delivers timely, actionable data directly into team workflows, reducing the need to check e

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Sphere OS version 25.12 is now available for evaluation
item.description: Azure Sphere OS version 25.12 is now available for evaluation in the Retail Eval feed. Whilst this release encompasses no customer-facing changes, it represents significant under-the-hood build system changes, as such, this release will operate for an ext

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: FIPS compliant mode for Application Gateway V2 SKUs
item.description: The Federal Information Processing Standard (FIPS) 140-2 is a US government standard that defines minimum security requirements for cryptographic modules in information technology products and systems. Azure Application Gateway V2 now supports FIPS mode,

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Zonal placement for Azure file shares Azure Files Premium LRS in select regions.
item.description: Zonal Placement for Azure Files Premium LRS is now GA in select regions! This capability gives you explicit control over zone locality by pinning storage accounts to a specific availability zone, enabling high availability, fault isolation, and predictabl

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Mistral Large 3 in Foundry
item.description: Mistral Large 3, a frontier-class open-weight model with Apache 2.0 licensing, is now available in Microsoft Foundry on Azure. This model delivers enterprise-grade reliability, long-context comprehension, and multimodal reasoning—making it ideal for produ

item.title: [Launched] Generally Availaible: Azure MCP Server support for Azure confidential ledger
item.description: The Azure MCP (Model Context Protocol) Server provides a streamlined approach for managing Azure resources—including Azure confidential ledger—through natural language prompts. This feature allows users to interact with confidential ledger without the nee

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Serverless workspaces in Azure Databricks
item.description: Serverless workspaces in Azure Databricks are now available in Public Preview, offering a fully managed workspace type that comes preconfigured with serverless compute and default storage to deliver an enterprise-ready SaaS experience with minimal infrast

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Blob Storage SFTP - Resumable Uploads
item.description: Announcing the general availability of resumable uploads for Azure Storage Blob SFTP. In the event of a failed partial transfer, this new feature enables users to open the partial file and continue writing the remaining content. This capability optimizes

item.title: Retirement: Remove dependency on these Azure ML SDKs before June 30, 2026
item.description: On June 30, 2026, AzureML SDK V1 will reach end of life. The following SDKs which work in tandem with the AzureML SDK V1 will also be retired. azureml-train-core azureml-pipeline azureml-pipeline-core azureml-pipeline-internal azureml-pipeline-steps

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server in Belgium Central
item.description: Now you can deploy Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server in the Belgium Central Azure region. Learn more.

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Update pg_squeeze to version 1.9.1 in Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server
item.description: Now you can upgrade the pg_squeeze extension to version 1.9.1 in Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server. Learn more.

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: The ip4r extension in Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server
item.description: Now you can install the ip4r extension in Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server. Ip4r provides efficient storage and indexing functionality for IPv4 and IPv6 addresses and ranges. Learn more.

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: The credcheck extension in Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server
item.description: Now you can install the credcheck extension in Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server. The credcheck extension helps you to enforce password and credential validation policies directly within PostgreSQL. Learn more.

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Load Balancer bandwidth metrics now support Protocol dimension
item.description: Bandwidth metrics for Azure Load Balancer are now published with metric dimension Protocol. When retrieving or viewing Byte, Packet, and SYN Count metrics in the Azure portal, you will now see the dimension Protocol. TCP traffic is denoted by Protocol=6,

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Perth - Azure Extended Zones
item.description: We are announcing the general availability of the Perth Azure Extended Zone. Azure Extended Zones are small-footprint extensions of Azure placed in metro areas, industry centers, or specific jurisdictions, to serve low latency and data residency workloa

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Model Context Protocol support in Azure API Management and Azure API Center
item.description: Announcing the public preview of Model Context Protocol (MCP) support in Azure API Management and Azure API Center. With this new capability, enterprises can transform their existing APIs into dynamic, agent-ready tools, while improving security and simpl

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Managed Identity support in Network Watcher VNET flow log, traffic analytics and packet capture
item.description: VNET flow log captures IP traffic flowing through VNETs, subnets, and NICs for monitoring, troubleshooting, network optimization, security, and compliance needs and stores the logs in Azure Storage Traffic Analytics leverages VNET flow logs and aggregates

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Claude Opus 4.5 Available in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Claude Opus 4.5 is now available in public preview on Microsoft Foundry, bringing advanced coding, agentic workflows, and enterprise productivity to Azure customers. Claude Opus 4.5 sets a new benchmark for performance and versatility, outperforming previ

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure File Sync in New Zealand North
item.description: Azure File Sync enables seamless tiering of data from on-premises Windows Servers to Azure Files for hybrid use cases and simplified migration. It also enables you to leverage the performance, flexibility and compatibility of your on-premises File Server

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Entra ID support for RDP connections
item.description: Announcing Azure Bastion now supports Microsoft Entra ID authentication for RDP connections to Windows VMs directly in the portal. This update brings two key benefits for RDP users connecting to their virtual machines: Enhanced Security: By eliminating lo

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Load Testing in Italy North
item.description: Azure Load Testing capability in Azure App Testing is now generally available in Italy North. Customers can now use Azure Load Testing, a fully managed load-testing service to generate high scale load and run simulations, easily identify performance bottl

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Regex support in T-SQL
item.description: Boost your SQL queries with powerful regular expressions (regex) support for advanced pattern matching. Now you can use native regex in T-SQL directly in Azure SQL and SQL Server 2025 queries—simplifying code, improving maintainability, and reducing depen

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure MCP Server for Azure Database for MySQL
item.description: Announcing that Azure MCP Server now supports Azure Database for MySQL. With this update, you can empower AI agents and applications to interact with your MySQL data using natural language through the open Model Context Protocol (MCP). Instead of writing

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Custom handler support in Azure Functions Flex consumption
item.description: Azure Functions now supports custom handlers in Flex consumption. Custom handlers are lightweight web servers that receive events from the Functions host. Any language that supports HTTP primitives can implement a custom handler. Custom handlers are best

item.title: Retirement: Migrate to dedicated VM for your compute clusters
item.description: On September 30, 2025, Low-Priority VM will reach end of life. However, you will continue to be supported on Azure Machine Learning until March 31, 2026. To ensure that your clusters are not automatically scaled down it is recommended to migrate to dedic

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Container network metrics filtering in Advanced Container Networking Services for (ACNS) for AKS
item.description: Container network metrics filtering in Azure Container Networking Services (ACNS) is now available in public preview. In containerized environments, the ingestion of excessive network metrics can lead to inflated storage costs and cluttered dashboards, ma

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: MCP support for AI toolchain operator add-on in AKS
item.description: MCP support for AI toolchain operator add-on is now Generally Available. The AI Toolchain Operator add-on now supports KAITO inference workspaces with integrated tool calling and Model Context Protocol (MCP), addressing key challenges around dynamic mo

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Cluster-wide Cilium network policy with Azure CNI powered by Cilium for AKS
item.description: Cluster-wide Cilium network policy for AKS clusters using Azure CNI powered by Cilium is now Generally Available. Managing consistent network policies across Kubernetes namespaces has long been a challenge for platform teams, especially in multi-tenant

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Local redirect policy in Azure CNI powered by Cilium for AKS
item.description: Local redirect policy with Azure CNI powered by Cilium for AKS is now generally available. Customers running high-scale AKS workloads often experience latency and performance bottlenecks due to cross-node traffic routing. The new local redirect policy add

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Layer 7 policy with Advanced Container Networking Services (ACNS) for AKS
item.description: To address the growing need for granular traffic control in microservices architectures, Azure Container Networking Services now offers Layer 7 Policy as a generally available feature. This capability empowers customers to enforce fine-grained traffic con

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure NetApp Files single file restore from backup
item.description: With Azure NetApp Files single file restore from backup, you can restore individual files from Azure NetApp Files backup vault without needing to restore an entire volume. By restoring only the necessary files, you can save time and cost needed for restor

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: DNS security policy Threat Intelligence feed
item.description: Azure DNS security policy Threat Intelligence feed is now generally available. Protect against known malicious domains with Microsoft’s managed Threat Intelligent feed. Smart Protection Almost all attacks begin with a DNS query. Threat Intelligen

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Sphere OS version 25.10 is now available
item.description: Azure Sphere OS version 25.10 is now available in the Retail feed. This release includes updates to Azure Sphere OS only; it does not include an updated SDK. If your devices are connected to the internet, they will receive the updated OS from the cloud.Th

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Trusted Launch is now supported for Arm64 Marketplace Images
item.description: Announcing that Trusted Launch supported Arm64 Images are now available in the Azure Marketplace. You can now get the dual benefit of cost and performance efficiencies of Arm64 in addition to the security benefits of Trusted Launch. Arm64 VMs bring cost

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure NetApp Files migration assistant (portal support)
item.description: Azure NetApp Files migration assistant (with SnapMirror) provides efficient and cost-effective data migration leveraging ONTAP's built-in replication engine for seamless transition from on-premises or CVO/other cloud providers to Azure NetApp Files (ANF).

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure NetApp Files cache volumes
item.description: Azure NetApp Files cache volumes are now available in public preview. The cache volumes feature is a new capability built on NetApp’s ONTAP FlexCache technology that provides a persistent, high-performance cache in Azure for data stored on ONTAP-based sto

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Monitor for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes with OpenShift and Azure Red Hat OpenShift
item.description: Azure Monitor provides a complete set of services for monitoring the health and performance of different layers of your Kubernetes infrastructure and the applications that depend on it.Azure Monitor now supports Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes with OpenShift

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Managed Lustre Support for Azure MCP Server
item.description: Azure Managed Lustre now offers full integration with Azure MCP Server, and this capability is generally available. Azure MCP Server enables developers and infrastructure teams to manage Azure resources—including Azure Managed Lustre file systems—using na

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: CSI Dynamic Provisioning for Azure Managed Lustre
item.description: CSI Dynamic Provisioning for Azure Managed Lustre (AMLFS) is now generally available. With dynamic provisioning, Kubernetes applications can request AMLFS-backed persistent volumes on demand using standard StorageClass and PersistentVolumeClaim objects—no

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: 20 MB/s/TiB Performance Tier for Azure Managed Lustre
item.description: Azure Managed Lustre now offers a new 20 MB/s/TiB performance tier in public preview, designed to meet the demands of large-scale AI and high-performance computing (HPC) workloads. This tier enables customers to provision file systems up to 25 PiB in a si

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Auto-import for Azure Managed Lustre
item.description: The Auto-import feature for Azure Managed Lustre (AMLFS) is now available in public preview. Auto-import enables seamless, policy-driven synchronization of data from Azure Blob Storage containers into AMLFS clusters, automatically reflecting new, changed,

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Recommended alerts for Azure Monitor Workspace
item.description: Azure Monitor now offers one-click enablement of recommended alerts directly in the Azure Portal for Azure Monitor Managed service for Prometheus customers. These alerts enable you to monitor the ingestion limits for Azure Monitor Workspace and avoid metr

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Managed Redis integration with Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Azure Managed Redis is now available as part of the Microsoft Foundry MCP tools catalog, allowing customers to use Redis as a knowledge store or memory store for AI agents. This integration makes it simple to connect Redis to Foundry-based agents, enablin

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: TLS and TCP termination on Azure Application Gateway
item.description: Azure Application Gateway has introduced general availability support for TLS and TCP protocol termination. This enhancement enables you to load balance and securely process non-HTTP(S) traffic for applications that rely on TCP or TLS protocols. The gatew
